title: Tephroseris phaeantha
taxonomy: Tephroseris phaeantha is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the flowering plants. The genus Tephroseris is part of the subfamily Senecio, which includes several related species. Tephroseris phaeantha is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. Its classification as a member of the Asteraceae family reflects its characteristics as a flowering perennial plant.

intro: Tephroseris phaeantha, also known as the Alpine Hawk's Beard, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in reducing symptoms associated with arthritis, as well as its ability to support the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Tephroseris phaeantha include sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
